evidence: `src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs:219-224` no longer overwrites `envelope.metadata.backend_used` or `envelope.metadata.model_used` after the adapter returns, so adapter-reported metadata now survives to downstream persistence.

`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:4647-4650` constructs `RecordProducer::Agent` from `envelope.metadata.backend_used.family` and `envelope.metadata.model_used.model_id`; the prompt-review, completion, and final-review persistence paths use the same envelope-metadata pattern.
`src/adapters/fs.rs:1105-1106` serializes the `ArtifactRecord` unchanged, so the populated `producer.backend_family` and `producer.model_id` are written into `history/artifacts/*.json`.

`src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1513-1605` contains an end-to-end regression asserting persisted artifact JSON uses envelope metadata instead of the configured target, and `src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs:401-437` adds focused coverage that the service preserves adapter-reported backend/model metadata.
`command -v cargo && command -v rustc && command -v rustfmt` returned no path in this environment, so executable Rust validation could not be performed.
findings_or_gaps: No functional defect was found in static review, but executable validation remains incomplete because the Rust toolchain (`cargo`, `rustc`, `rustfmt`) is unavailable here.
follow_up_or_amendments: Run `cargo test invoke_preserves_adapter_reported_backend_and_model_metadata persisted_artifacts_use_envelope_metadata_not_resolved_target` or the relevant Rust test suite once the toolchain is available.
